English Sentence: She has been working at the same company for over ten years and was recently promoted to senior manager.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 佢最近升咗做高級經理。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect continuous + recently promoted

English Sentence: He decided to leave his job and start his own business after years of saving and planning.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 佢決定辭職自己創業。
Grammar / Usage Note: after + gerund phrase for sequence

English Sentence: I have a meeting with my manager this afternoon to discuss my performance review.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我今日下晝要同經理開會。
Grammar / Usage Note: present arrangement for near future

English Sentence: She found the job through a recruitment agency and started the following week.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她通過招聘機構找到了這份工作，並在下週開始上班。
Grammar / Usage Note: past sequence with following

English Sentence: He works well under pressure and always delivers high-quality work on time.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他在壓力下工作表現很好，總是能準時交出高品質的工作成果。
Grammar / Usage Note: positive professional traits

English Sentence: I applied for the position online and received a call for an interview two days later.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我在網上申請了這個職位，兩天後接到了面試電話。
Grammar / Usage Note: past sequence with time reference

English Sentence: She is responsible for managing a team of fifteen people and overseeing all project timelines.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她負責管理一個十五人的團隊，並監督所有專案時間表。
Grammar / Usage Note: be responsible for + gerund list

English Sentence: He was made redundant last year but managed to find a new job within three months.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他去年被裁員，但在三個月內設法找到了新工作。
Grammar / Usage Note: past passive with but managed to

English Sentence: I find working from home much more productive as there are fewer distractions.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我發現在家工作效率更高，因為干擾較少。
Grammar / Usage Note: I find + comparative + as clause for reason

English Sentence: She negotiated a higher salary before accepting the job offer.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她在接受工作邀請之前協商了更高的薪資。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple with before + gerund

English Sentence: He takes his professional development seriously and attends at least two training courses a year.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他非常重視自己的職業發展，每年至少參加兩次培訓課程。
Grammar / Usage Note: takes seriously + frequency expression

English Sentence: I gave my resignation with one month's notice as required by my contract.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我按照合約要求，提前一個月提出了辭職。
Grammar / Usage Note: as required by for formal reason

English Sentence: She collaborates well with her colleagues and is always willing to share her knowledge.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她與同事合作得很好，總是樂於分享她的知識。
Grammar / Usage Note: positive professional trait with always willing

English Sentence: He has just been offered a position at a company overseas and is considering whether to accept.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他剛剛獲得了一家海外公司的職位邀請，正在考慮是否接受。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect just + present continuous considering

English Sentence: Working in a fast-paced environment has taught me to prioritise tasks effectively.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 在快節奏的環境中工作讓我學會了有效地安排任務優先順序。
Grammar / Usage Note: gerund subject + taught me to

English Sentence: She is passionate about her work and often goes above and beyond what is expected.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她對自己的工作充滿熱情，常常超出預期地付出。
Grammar / Usage Note: go above and beyond for extra effort

English Sentence: I have been commuting for two hours every day, which is one of the reasons I want to work remotely.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我每天通勤兩個小時，這是我想要遠距工作的原因之一。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect continuous + which clause

English Sentence: He built strong professional relationships throughout his career that have opened many doors for him.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他在整個職業生涯中建立了牢固的職業關係，這為他打開了很多大門。
Grammar / Usage Note: that relative clause for career benefit

English Sentence: The company offers excellent benefits including health insurance, annual leave, and flexible working hours.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這家公司提供優良的福利，包括健康保險、年假和彈性工作時間。
Grammar / Usage Note: including + list for company benefits

English Sentence: She keeps a record of her achievements so that she is prepared for her annual performance review.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她記錄自己的成就，以便為年度績效考核做好準備。
Grammar / Usage Note: so that clause for purpose

English Sentence: She studied hard for months before her exams and was delighted when she passed.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她在考試前努力學習了幾個月，通過考試時非常高興。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple sequence with emotional result

English Sentence: He graduated with a first-class degree in computer science and immediately received several job offers.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他以電腦科學一等學位畢業，並立即收到了幾份工作邀請。
Grammar / Usage Note: graduate with a degree + immediate result

English Sentence: I am taking an online course in data analysis to improve my career prospects.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我正在上一門數據分析的線上課程，以改善我的職業前景。
Grammar / Usage Note: present continuous with infinitive of purpose

English Sentence: She was the first person in her family to go to university, which was a huge achievement.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她是家族中第一個上大學的人，這是一項巨大的成就。
Grammar / Usage Note: first person to + infinitive with which clause

English Sentence: He found the first year of university very challenging but gradually adapted to the workload.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他發現大學第一年非常具有挑戰性，但逐漸適應了課業量。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple with but + gradually for adjustment

English Sentence: I have been revising for my final exams and have not had much time for anything else.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我一直在為期末考試複習，沒有太多時間做其他事情。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect continuous + present perfect negative

English Sentence: She received a scholarship that covered her tuition fees and living expenses for three years.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她獲得了一筆獎學金，支付了她三年的學費和生活費用。
Grammar / Usage Note: that clause specifying what the scholarship covered

English Sentence: He is studying for a professional qualification alongside his full-time job.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他一邊做全職工作，一邊攻讀專業資格。
Grammar / Usage Note: present continuous with alongside for dual activity

English Sentence: I struggled with maths at school but later discovered a passion for statistics at university.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我在學校時數學很吃力，但後來在大學發現了對統計學的熱情。
Grammar / Usage Note: contrast with but + later discovery

English Sentence: She chose her university based on its strong reputation in her field of study.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她根據大學在她研究領域的良好聲譽來選擇學校。
Grammar / Usage Note: based on + reason for decision

English Sentence: He dropped out of his course in the first year but went back to study five years later.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他在第一年就退學了，但五年後重返學習。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple with contrasting but

English Sentence: The teacher explained the concept so clearly that every student in the class understood it.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 老師把這個概念解釋得非常清楚，班上每個學生都理解了。
Grammar / Usage Note: so... that for result

English Sentence: I realised that I had chosen the wrong course after my first semester and transferred to a different one.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我在第一個學期後意識到我選錯了課程，並轉到了另一個課程。
Grammar / Usage Note: past perfect + past simple for discovery and action

English Sentence: She was awarded a distinction for her dissertation, which was published in an academic journal.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她的畢業論文獲得了優異的成績，並被發表在一本學術期刊上。
Grammar / Usage Note: passive with which clause for further outcome

English Sentence: He attends evening classes after work three times a week to learn a second language.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他每週三次在下班後參加夜間課程學習第二語言。
Grammar / Usage Note: frequency + after work for part-time study routine

English Sentence: I find group study sessions much more effective than studying alone.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我發現小組學習比單獨學習有效得多。
Grammar / Usage Note: I find + comparative + than

English Sentence: She applied to several universities and was offered places at all of them.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她申請了幾所大學，並獲得了所有學校的錄取通知。
Grammar / Usage Note: past passive with all of them

English Sentence: He is the most dedicated student in the class and always submits his work early.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他是班上最認真的學生，總是提前提交作業。
Grammar / Usage Note: superlative with always for habit

English Sentence: Taking a gap year before university gave her time to gain valuable work experience.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 大學前的間隔年給了她時間積累寶貴的工作經驗。
Grammar / Usage Note: gerund subject + gave time to

English Sentence: I wish I had studied harder when I was younger because it would have made a big difference.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我希望年輕的時候更努力學習，因為那會帶來很大的不同。
Grammar / Usage Note: I wish + past perfect for regret

English Sentence: She has been feeling exhausted lately and her doctor advised her to take a few days off work.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她最近一直感到精疲力竭，她的醫生建議她休息幾天不工作。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect continuous + advised to

English Sentence: He broke his wrist in a cycling accident and needed surgery to repair the damage.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他在騎自行車事故中摔斷了手腕，需要手術修復損傷。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple sequence with needed to

English Sentence: I have been trying to eat more healthily by cutting out processed food and cooking from scratch.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我一直在嘗試通過不吃加工食品和從頭開始烹飪來吃得更健康。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect continuous + by + gerund

English Sentence: She was diagnosed with high blood pressure and was told to reduce her salt intake.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她被診斷出患有高血壓，並被告知要減少鹽分攝取。
Grammar / Usage Note: past passive diagnosis + told to

English Sentence: He recovered from his illness much more quickly than the doctors had expected.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他從疾病中康復的速度比醫生預期的要快得多。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple + comparative + past perfect

English Sentence: I have started going to the gym three times a week since my doctor told me to exercise more.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 自從醫生告訴我多運動以來，我已經開始每週去三次健身房了。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect + since + past simple trigger

English Sentence: She maintains a healthy lifestyle by eating well, sleeping enough, and managing her stress levels.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她通過飲食均衡、睡眠充足和管理壓力水準來保持健康的生活方式。
Grammar / Usage Note: by + gerund list for method

English Sentence: He has been on medication for six months and his condition has improved significantly.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他已經服藥六個月了，他的狀況顯著改善了。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect continuous + present perfect result

English Sentence: I had a health scare last year that made me completely rethink my lifestyle choices.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我去年有過一次健康危機，讓我完全重新思考了我的生活方式選擇。
Grammar / Usage Note: that clause for impact of event

English Sentence: She goes for a medical check-up every year to make sure everything is fine.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她每年都去做一次健康檢查，以確保一切正常。
Grammar / Usage Note: frequency + purpose with to + infinitive

English Sentence: He is allergic to penicillin, so he always tells his doctor before taking any medication.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他對青黴素過敏，所以他在服用任何藥物之前總是告訴醫生。
Grammar / Usage Note: so clause for consequence of allergy

English Sentence: I find that regular exercise helps me manage my stress and sleep much better at night.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我發現定期運動幫助我管理壓力，並在夜間睡得更好。
Grammar / Usage Note: I find that + helps me + infinitive

English Sentence: She had knee surgery earlier this year and has been going to physiotherapy twice a week since then.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她今年早些時候做了膝蓋手術，從那以後每週做兩次物理治療。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple + present perfect continuous since then

English Sentence: He eats a balanced diet, avoids alcohol, and exercises regularly, which is why he looks so healthy.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他飲食均衡、避免飲酒並定期運動，這就是他看起來那麼健康的原因。
Grammar / Usage Note: list + which is why clause

English Sentence: I try to get at least seven hours of sleep each night because I notice a huge difference in how I feel.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我每晚嘗試至少睡七個小時，因為我注意到這對我的感覺有很大的影響。
Grammar / Usage Note: try to + at least + because clause

English Sentence: She was told that her cholesterol was too high and that she needed to change her diet immediately.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她被告知她的膽固醇太高了，需要立即改變飲食。
Grammar / Usage Note: past passive reporting with that clauses

English Sentence: He uses meditation and breathing exercises to manage anxiety without relying on medication.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他使用冥想和呼吸練習來管理焦慮，而不依賴藥物。
Grammar / Usage Note: without + gerund for alternative method

English Sentence: I booked an appointment with my GP as soon as I noticed the symptoms.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我一注意到症狀就立即預約了全科醫生。
Grammar / Usage Note: as soon as + past simple for prompt action

English Sentence: She has been following a plant-based diet for two years and says she has never felt better.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她遵循植物性飲食已有兩年，她說她從未感覺這麼好過。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect continuous + says + present perfect

English Sentence: He takes preventative health seriously and gets all recommended vaccinations and screenings.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他非常重視預防保健，接受所有推薦的疫苗接種和篩檢。
Grammar / Usage Note: takes seriously + and for compound habit

English Sentence: I have always wanted to visit Japan; the culture, food, and scenery look absolutely stunning.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我一直想去日本；那裡的文化、食物和風景看起來令人嘆為觀止。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect always wanted + semi-colon detail

English Sentence: She booked the flights months in advance to get a better deal.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她提前幾個月訂了機票，以獲得更好的價格。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple with to + infinitive of purpose

English Sentence: We got completely lost on our first day in the city, but ended up discovering some amazing places.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們在城市的第一天完全迷路了，但最終發現了一些令人驚奇的地方。
Grammar / Usage Note: but ended up + gerund for unexpected positive outcome

English Sentence: He prefers travelling off-season to avoid the crowds and to benefit from lower prices.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他更喜歡在淡季旅行，以避免人潮並享受較低的價格。
Grammar / Usage Note: prefer + gerund + to + infinitive list

English Sentence: I always research a destination thoroughly before I travel so that I make the most of my time.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我在旅行前總是徹底研究目的地，以充分利用我的時間。
Grammar / Usage Note: always + before clause + so that purpose

English Sentence: She packed light for her trip, bringing only a carry-on bag to avoid checked luggage fees.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她輕裝旅行，只帶了一個手提行李，以避免托運行李費用。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple with only + infinitive of purpose

English Sentence: We stayed in a small family-run guesthouse which gave us a more authentic experience of the local culture.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們住在一家小型家庭式旅館，這讓我們對當地文化有了更真實的體驗。
Grammar / Usage Note: which clause for resulting experience

English Sentence: He has been to over thirty countries and says that every trip teaches him something new.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他去過三十多個國家，他說每次旅行都能讓他學到新東西。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect + says + present simple with every

English Sentence: I missed my connecting flight because my first flight was delayed by two hours.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我錯過了轉機航班，因為第一班飛機延誤了兩個小時。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple + because clause with passive

English Sentence: She always travels with travel insurance because she believes it is not worth the risk.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她旅行時總是購買旅遊保險，因為她認為冒那個風險不值得。
Grammar / Usage Note: always + because + believe clause

English Sentence: We rented a car and drove along the coast, stopping at small villages along the way.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們租了一輛車沿著海岸線開，沿途在小村莊停留。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple with present participle for manner

English Sentence: He learned a few basic phrases in the local language before his trip, which the locals really appreciated.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他出發前學了幾句當地語言的基本短語，當地人對此非常讚賞。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple + which clause for positive reaction

English Sentence: I find that solo travel forces you to be more independent and open to new experiences.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我發現獨自旅行迫使你更獨立，並對新的體驗更加開放。
Grammar / Usage Note: I find that + forces you to for insight

English Sentence: She spent three months backpacking across Southeast Asia after finishing her degree.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她完成學業後，花了三個月的時間背包遊覽東南亞。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple + after + gerund

English Sentence: We were upgraded to business class on our flight home, which was a wonderful surprise.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們在回家的航班上被升等到商務艙，這是一個美妙的驚喜。
Grammar / Usage Note: passive upgrade + which clause

English Sentence: He always keeps copies of his important documents in a separate bag when he travels.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他旅行時總是把重要文件的副本放在另一個包裡。
Grammar / Usage Note: always + when clause for safety habit

English Sentence: I try to travel as sustainably as possible by choosing direct flights and staying in eco-friendly accommodation.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我盡量以盡可能可持續的方式旅行，選擇直飛航班並住在環保住宿。
Grammar / Usage Note: try to + by + gerund list for method

English Sentence: She fell in love with the city on her first visit and moved there permanently two years later.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她在第一次訪問這座城市時就愛上了它，兩年後她永久搬到了那裡。
Grammar / Usage Note: fell in love + and past simple sequence

English Sentence: The trip was not what we had expected, but it turned out to be one of our best holidays ever.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這次旅行不是我們所預期的，但它結果成為我們有史以來最好的假期之一。
Grammar / Usage Note: not what + past perfect + turned out to be

English Sentence: He made a point of trying the local street food wherever he went, no matter how unusual it looked.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他特意在每到一個地方都嘗試當地的街頭食物，無論看起來多麼不尋常。
Grammar / Usage Note: made a point of + gerund + no matter how

English Sentence: She compared prices across several websites before making her final decision.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她在做最終決定之前比較了幾個網站的價格。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple + before + gerund

English Sentence: He always sticks to a budget when he goes food shopping to avoid overspending.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他去採買食物時總是嚴格遵守預算，以避免超支。
Grammar / Usage Note: always + when clause + to + infinitive

English Sentence: I returned the jacket because it was the wrong size, and the shop gave me a full refund.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我退了那件夾克，因為尺寸不對，商店給了我全額退款。
Grammar / Usage Note: past sequence with because + result

English Sentence: She is saving up to buy a new laptop and tries not to spend money on things she doesn't need.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她正在存錢買新筆記型電腦，並盡量不在不需要的東西上花錢。
Grammar / Usage Note: present continuous + tries not to

English Sentence: He bought the concert tickets months in advance because they sold out quickly last year.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他提前幾個月買了演唱會門票，因為去年門票很快就售罄了。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple with because + past simple explanation

English Sentence: I always check reviews before buying anything online to make sure the quality is good.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我在網上購買任何東西之前總是查看評論，以確保品質良好。
Grammar / Usage Note: always + before + gerund + to + infinitive

English Sentence: She found a great second-hand sofa online and paid half the price of a new one.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她在網上找到了一張很棒的二手沙發，只花了新的一半的價錢。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple with comparative price

English Sentence: He has been trying to cut down on unnecessary spending since he set a savings goal.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 自從他設定了儲蓄目標以來，他一直在嘗試減少不必要的支出。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect continuous + since clause

English Sentence: I bought too many things during the sale and later regretted spending so much.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我在特賣期間買了太多東西，後來後悔花了那麼多錢。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple + later + past simple regret

English Sentence: She uses a budgeting app to track her expenses and has saved significantly since she started using it.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她使用預算應用程式來追蹤支出，自從她開始使用它以來已經節省了大量資金。
Grammar / Usage Note: present simple + present perfect since

English Sentence: He always pays his credit card bill in full each month to avoid paying interest.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他每個月都會全額支付信用卡帳單，以避免支付利息。
Grammar / Usage Note: always + to + infinitive for financial habit

English Sentence: I was shocked at how much I had spent when I reviewed my bank statement at the end of the month.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 當我在月底查看銀行對帳單時，我對自己花了多少錢感到震驚。
Grammar / Usage Note: was shocked at + how much + past perfect

English Sentence: She negotiated a lower price when she bought her car by doing research beforehand.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她在購車時通過事先做研究協商到了更低的價格。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple + by + gerund for method

English Sentence: He prefers paying in cash rather than using a card because it helps him spend less.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他更喜歡用現金付款而不是使用卡，因為這幫助他花費更少。
Grammar / Usage Note: prefer + gerund + because clause

English Sentence: I set up an automatic transfer to my savings account on payday so that I save before I spend.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我在發薪日設置了自動轉帳到我的儲蓄帳戶，這樣我就能在花費之前先儲蓄。
Grammar / Usage Note: set up automatic transfer + so that clause

English Sentence: She uses her smartphone for almost everything, from banking to ordering food and navigating the city.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她幾乎用智慧型手機做所有事情，從銀行業務到訂餐以及在城市中導航。
Grammar / Usage Note: from... to for listing uses

English Sentence: He is very careful about his online privacy and never shares personal information on unfamiliar websites.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他非常注重自己的網路隱私，從不在不熟悉的網站上分享個人資訊。
Grammar / Usage Note: is careful about + and never clause

English Sentence: I find it difficult to switch off from work because I am always connected to my email.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我發現很難從工作中抽身，因為我總是和電子郵件保持連線。
Grammar / Usage Note: I find it difficult + because + always

English Sentence: She teaches herself new software by watching tutorial videos online in her spare time.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她在空閒時間通過觀看網路教學影片自學新軟體。
Grammar / Usage Note: teaches herself + by + gerund

English Sentence: He had his identity stolen online and had to spend months sorting out the damage.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他的身份在網路上被盜，不得不花幾個月的時間處理損失。
Grammar / Usage Note: past passive + had to + gerund

English Sentence: I back up all my important files to the cloud every week in case my computer crashes.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我每週都將所有重要文件備份到雲端，以防我的電腦崩潰。
Grammar / Usage Note: frequency + in case clause for precaution

English Sentence: She has been following the latest developments in artificial intelligence with great interest.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她一直在密切關注人工智慧的最新發展。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect continuous for ongoing interest

English Sentence: He prefers using a laptop for work and a tablet for reading and entertainment.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他更喜歡用筆記型電腦工作，用平板電腦閱讀和娛樂。
Grammar / Usage Note: prefer + gerund + and for parallel structure

English Sentence: I deleted my social media accounts because I found them too distracting and time-consuming.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我刪除了我的社群媒體帳戶，因為我發現它們太分散注意力且耗時。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple + because I found clause

English Sentence: She uses video calling to stay in touch with her family who live overseas.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她使用視訊通話與住在海外的家人保持聯繫。
Grammar / Usage Note: to + infinitive for purpose + who clause

English Sentence: He set up two-factor authentication on all his accounts after his email was hacked.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 在他的電子郵件被駭客入侵後，他在所有帳戶上設置了雙重驗證。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple + after + passive past simple

English Sentence: I try to limit the amount of time I spend on my phone to two hours a day outside of work.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我嘗試將工作以外的時間使用手機限制在每天兩小時以內。
Grammar / Usage Note: try to limit + to + time expression

English Sentence: She learned to code online during the pandemic and now works as a junior developer.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她在疫情期間在網上學習了編程，現在作為初級開發人員工作。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple + and now + present simple

English Sentence: He is concerned about how much personal data companies collect without users' knowledge.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他擔心企業在用戶不知情的情況下收集了多少個人資料。
Grammar / Usage Note: is concerned about + how much clause

English Sentence: I use a password manager to create and store strong, unique passwords for every account.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我使用密碼管理器為每個帳戶創建和存儲強而獨特的密碼。
Grammar / Usage Note: to + infinitive list for tool purpose

English Sentence: I realised there was a problem when I noticed the numbers didn't add up.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我注意到數字對不上時意識到有問題。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple sequence with when clause

English Sentence: She stayed calm and thought through the options carefully before making a decision.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她保持冷靜，在做決定之前仔細考慮了各種選擇。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple + before + gerund

English Sentence: He asked for help as soon as he realised he couldn't solve the problem on his own.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他一意識到自己無法獨立解決問題就立即尋求了幫助。
Grammar / Usage Note: as soon as + past simple for prompt action

English Sentence: I broke the problem down into smaller steps, which made it much easier to manage.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我把問題分解成更小的步驟，這讓管理起來容易多了。
Grammar / Usage Note: broke down into + which clause for benefit

English Sentence: She contacted the customer service team immediately and the issue was resolved within an hour.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她立即聯繫了客服團隊，問題在一個小時內得到了解決。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple + passive resolution

English Sentence: He tried several different approaches before he finally found one that worked.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他嘗試了幾種不同的方法，最終找到了一個有效的方法。
Grammar / Usage Note: before + finally for persistence

English Sentence: I had to think on my feet when the original plan fell through at the last minute.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 當原計劃在最後一刻失敗時，我不得不臨機應變。
Grammar / Usage Note: think on my feet for adaptability

English Sentence: She set up a meeting to discuss the issue openly so that everyone could share their perspective.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她安排了一次會議公開討論這個問題，以便每個人都能分享自己的觀點。
Grammar / Usage Note: so that + could for collective purpose

English Sentence: He identified the root cause of the problem by analysing the data carefully.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他通過仔細分析數據找出了問題的根本原因。
Grammar / Usage Note: by + gerund for method

English Sentence: I should have double-checked the information before sending the report.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我應該在發送報告之前再次確認那些資訊。
Grammar / Usage Note: should have + past participle for regret

English Sentence: She came up with a creative solution that saved the company both time and money.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她想出了一個創造性的解決方案，為公司節省了時間和金錢。
Grammar / Usage Note: came up with + that clause for impact

English Sentence: He remained positive throughout the process, which helped keep the team motivated.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他在整個過程中保持積極，這有助於保持團隊的積極性。
Grammar / Usage Note: remained + which clause for team impact

English Sentence: I learned from the mistake and put measures in place to make sure it doesn't happen again.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我從這個錯誤中學習，並採取了措施確保它不會再次發生。
Grammar / Usage Note: learned from + put measures in place

English Sentence: She delegated different parts of the problem to the most suitable team members.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她將問題的不同部分分配給了最合適的團隊成員。
Grammar / Usage Note: delegate + to most suitable for

English Sentence: He always considers the long-term consequences before making any major decision.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他在做任何重大決定之前總是考慮長期後果。
Grammar / Usage Note: always + before + gerund for cautious habit
